recent_calls.summary: The user acknowledged receiving a call from the company and the agent attempted to assist but then transferred the call to a live agent for further help. The call ended with a successful transfer.

Edward from American Home Shield called to confirm if any repair work had been done for an air conditioning dispatch at a member's address, as the member plans to use their own service provider. The agent explained they do not have live repair status but assured Edward that the request would be passed to the team for follow-up. Edward requested immediate information, but the agent confirmed a team member would contact him shortly with the repair status.

The user called to inquire about an air conditioning estimate and why it was not submitted to their warranty company. The agent collected the user's information and confirmed a follow-up would be arranged as soon as possible. The user declined information about maintenance programs and feedback requests.
The user, Patrick, called and requested to speak to someone. The agent, Sophie, promptly transferred the call to a live agent for further assistance.
The user requested to speak with a live representative, and the agent successfully transferred the call to another agent. The call ended shortly after the transfer was completed.
The user requested to speak with a live person, and the agent promptly transferred the call to another live agent. The transfer was successful, and the call ended shortly after.

The user called to confirm the timing of a repair appointment after receiving a text indicating an earlier arrival window. The agent verified that the appointment is scheduled for April 10th between 12:00 and 1:00 p.m., confirming the user has not missed it. The user was satisfied with the information and did not request any changes or additional services.

The user called to confirm the time of their appointment, initially thinking it was at 10:00 AM. The agent clarified that the appointment is actually scheduled between 12:00 and 1:00 PM with technician Devon. The user confirmed and ended the call without further questions.

The user called to reschedule a furnace installation service originally set for April 14th due to a guest checkout timing conflict. They requested to move the service to around 11 AM after the guest checks out. The user confirmed the service is through a home warranty and provided an estimate number but did not have the dispatch number. The agent gathered necessary details and confirmed the installation specifics.
The user returned a call from Budget and was quickly transferred to a live agent for further assistance. The initial agent facilitated the transfer without engaging in a detailed conversation.
The call was very brief and disconnected abruptly with the user not engaging fully with the agent. The user indicated they did not need assistance and hung up quickly.

Victor called to clarify which items were sent to his home warranty company for approval regarding his outdoor AC unit and evaporator coil replacement. The agent confirmed the details and assured Victor that someone from Budget Heating, Cooling & Plumbing would follow up to clarify the estimates sent to the warranty company. Victor expressed satisfaction with the call and declined additional maintenance program information as he already had it.
The call was very brief, with the agent greeting the user and asking how to assist, but the user hung up immediately without responding.

Virginia Slackman called to inquire about the timing of her AC technician's appointment, which she believed was scheduled between 8 and 10 AM. The agent found an appointment for noon to 1 PM and acknowledged the discrepancy, promising to relay the information to the team. The call ended with the user requesting to speak to a live agent, and the call was successfully transferred.

Leanne from American Home Shield called to update a dispatch for Judd Kennedy regarding issues with both the AC unit and furnace replacement. The agent confirmed the details and noted the customer's preference for follow-up within the day. The user declined information about maintenance programs and provided positive feedback about the call.

The user called to schedule a service appointment for a non-working HVAC unit, preferring tomorrow morning at 8:00 a.m. The agent collected all necessary details and informed the user that a human agent will follow up to confirm availability. The call ended with the user acknowledging the information and thanking the agent.

The user called to schedule a service appointment for an air conditioning unit under a home warranty. The agent confirmed the dispatch number and service details, and the user provided positive feedback about the interaction. The agent assured the user that the team would follow up to confirm the appointment.
The user requested to speak to Amy, and the agent successfully transferred the call to the appropriate person. The call ended shortly after the transfer was completed.

The user, Tanya from Jefferson County code enforcement, called to request the immediate cancellation of an HVAC inspection for a permit at 24004 Hilltop Drive, stating the address is not in their jurisdiction. The agent confirmed the details and assured that the cancellation request would be sent to the team, with a confirmation to follow. The user declined information about maintenance programs and feedback requests.

The user called to inquire about a cooling service estimate they received via email and wanted clarification on whether payment was required. The agent gathered relevant details and then transferred the call to a live representative for further assistance.

The user, Tanya from Jefferson County code enforcement, called to speak with Daniel Lagarse regarding permit number 26-0367 at 2004 Hilltop Drive. The agent informed her that Daniel was unavailable due to after-hours and took a message for follow-up on the next business day. Tanya provided her contact information and ended the call after confirming no further assistance was needed.

The user called to reschedule an existing air conditioning thermostat appointment to Tuesday, April 7, 2026, with a preferred time window between 1:30 PM and 10 PM. The agent confirmed the details, including the user's name, phone number, and service address, and collected feedback on the AI assistant experience. The call ended successfully with no issues.
The call was very brief, with the agent greeting the user and asking how to assist, but the user hung up immediately without responding.

Nathan from American Home Shield called to discuss a recent service on a member's air conditioning unit that is not holding Freon. The agent confirmed details and arranged for Budget Heating, Cooling & Plumbing to follow up with the member as soon as possible next business day. The user provided feedback rating the AI assistant experience as a 4 out of 5.

The user called after hours seeking information about an appointment for the evening. The agent informed the user that appointment details cannot be confirmed or updated after hours and that the team will follow up on the next business day. The call ended shortly after without further action.

The user, Richard, called to schedule an air conditioner service appointment related to a new thermostat for the next day after 3 PM. The agent confirmed the details and informed Richard that a representative would reach out the next business day to confirm the timing. Richard declined to provide additional feedback and ended the call.

The user called Budget's after-hours service but decided to try again in the morning without providing any service details. The agent courteously ended the call, inviting the user to call back if needed.

The user called to check on an estimate but the agent, Evie, was unable to access specific details due to after-hours. The agent assured the user that the team would follow up on the next business day.

The user, Kristen, called to schedule a regular service appointment for a cooling issue involving a leak at the base of the furnace. She requested the appointment for Wednesday after 10 AM and confirmed her service address and contact number. The agent confirmed the details and offered to collect feedback, which Kristen declined.

The user, Stacy Teak, called back to schedule a callback for a cooling service appointment at 2808 McNair, Saint Louis. The agent confirmed the details and assured Stacy that someone from Budget would reach out the next business day to assist with scheduling the appointment. Stacy declined additional information about maintenance programs and feedback requests.

Trisha Stratton called to report a flooding basement caused by a broken water valve and a disconnected water softener. The agent documented the urgent issue and flagged it for follow-up by the team on the next business day, but could not provide immediate assistance due to after-hours. The user declined further follow-up at the moment.
The call was very brief and disconnected shortly after the agent introduced themselves and asked for the user's ZIP code. The user did not provide any clear response.

The user requested to speak with a manager during an after-hours call. The agent informed the user that a manager could not be connected immediately but assured the request would be forwarded to the management team on the next business day.

The user returned a call from Budget and initially wanted to schedule AC servicing. However, after realizing they were speaking with a virtual assistant and not a live person, the user decided to cancel all requests but requested a live person to call them the next day. The agent confirmed the cancellation and arranged for a team member to call the user the following day.
The user called to speak to someone named Mister Hello but did not respond after the agent's prompt for more information. The call ended shortly after with no further interaction.

The user called to schedule an emergency air conditioner service under a home warranty. The agent collected all necessary details including the user's name, address, phone number, and dispatch number, and confirmed the service request. The user expressed interest in receiving information about home maintenance programs, and the call ended with the agent confirming that someone would contact the user shortly.

The user, Sally from Rennie National Accounts, called to inquire about the status of a quote for a specific address. The agent attempted to assist but transferred the call to a live agent for further help. The call ended with a successful transfer.
The call was very brief, with the agent greeting the user and asking how to assist, but the user hung up immediately without responding.
The call was very brief, with the agent greeting the user and asking how to assist. The user hung up immediately without responding or engaging further.

Benjamin called to get an update on his upcoming appointment, but the agent could not find the appointment in the system. Benjamin requested to be transferred to a live representative, and the agent successfully transferred the call.

The user called to confirm the date of their appointment, which the agent verified as Thursday, April 9th, between 2:00 and 4:00 p.m. The user had no further questions or updates and ended the call politely. The agent confirmed the appointment details multiple times to ensure clarity.

The user called to schedule a service appointment for a furnace gas valve replacement. The agent booked the appointment for April 9th at 2:00 p.m. and confirmed the user's details. The user was satisfied and provided positive feedback about the assistant's time and help.
The user called to return a phone call and was promptly transferred to a live agent for further assistance. The agent confirmed the user's need and successfully completed the call transfer.

The user called about a furnace issue involving a gas valve and a gas smell, stating that emergency services had already been contacted earlier that day. The agent advised the user to call 911 and the gas company due to the gas leak, but the user insisted these steps had already been taken. The call ended with some miscommunication between the user and the agent.

The user called to inquire about the arrival time of the serviceman, Michael, who was scheduled between 11:00 a.m. and 12:00 p.m. The user reported that it was almost 2:30 p.m. and the technician had not arrived. The agent apologized for the delay and transferred the call to a live agent for further assistance.
The call was very brief and disconnected almost immediately after the agent began speaking. No meaningful conversation or information exchange occurred.

The user reported water collecting in the pan under their water heater and requested a technician to inspect it. The agent scheduled an appointment for 8:00 a.m. the next day and confirmed the user wanted a call before the visit to discuss if the issue was normal and warranty coverage. The user was satisfied with the service and gave positive feedback.

The user called after receiving a call about an appointment but was unsure about the details or the service type. The agent attempted to clarify the issue but the user wanted to speak to a live person instead of the AI assistant. The call was then transferred to a live agent as requested.

Kara called to schedule a heating service appointment for her home warranty. The agent confirmed the issue and property details, offered available time slots, and successfully booked an appointment for Wednesday at 4:00 p.m. The call ended with confirmation of the booking and information about the dispatch fee.
